content:
I'll split this bead into 4 focused child beads, ordered from fundamental infrastructure fixes to specific test stabilization.

Let me create the child beads:

args.command:
br create "Fix test isolation and state reset across integration tests" "Fix fundamental state leak issues that affect all integration tests.

Issues:
- Tests that share state between test runs
- Database/external service state not properly reset
- Mock/stub state leaking between tests

Files affected:
- ALL integration tests (will benefit from proper isolation)

Acceptance criteria:
- Each integration test resets all state before running
- No shared state between test runs
- Tests can run independently in any order
- Tests pass on 3+ consecutive runs" --label split-child
args.description: Create first child bead for test isolation

output:
Exit code 2
error: unexpected argument 'Fix test isolation and state reset across integration tests' found

Usage: br create [OPTIONS] --title <TITLE>

For more information, try '--help'.

Create a new bead

Usage: br create [OPTIONS] --title <TITLE>

Options:
      --title <TITLE>              Bead title
  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>      Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
      --type <TYPE>                Bead type [default: task]
      --priority <PRIORITY>        Priority (0=Critical, 4=Backlog) [default: 2]
      --description <DESCRIPTION>  Description
      --assignee <ASSIGNEE>        Assignee
      --label <LABEL>              Labels
  -h, --he

args.command:
br create --title "Fix test isolation and state reset" --description "Fix fundamental state leak issues that affect all integration tests.

Issues:
- Tests that share state between test runs
- Database/external service state not properly reset
- Mock/stub state leaking between tests

Files affected:
- ALL integration tests (will benefit from proper isolation)

Acceptance criteria:
- Each integration test resets all state before running
- No shared state between test runs
- Tests can run independently in any order
- Tests pass on 3+ consecutive runs" --label split-child
args.description: Create first child bead for test isolation

output: bf-3had3

args.command:
br create --title "Fix time-based assertions with deterministic time" --description "Fix time-dependent tests that fail due to non-deterministic time.

Issues:
- password-reset.test.ts uses real time for token expiry checks
- Time-based assertions can fail depending on when tests run
- Tests relying on Date.now() or setTimeout timing

Files to fix:
- packages/server/src/integration/password-reset.test.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- All time-based assertions use fake timers or deterministic time
- Password reset expiry tests are deterministic
- No tests rely on real system time" --label split-child
args.description: Create second child bead for time-based tests

output: bf-28zxk

Exit code 2
error: unrecognized subcommand 'add-dependency'

  tip: a similar subcommand exists: 'dep'

Usage: br [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

For more information, try '--help'.

Manage dependencies

Usage: br dep [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  add     Add a dependency
  remove  Remove a dependency
  list    List dependencies of an issue
  tree    Show dependency tree rooted at issue
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>  Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
  -h, --help                   Print help

args.command:
br create --title "Fix Hono app instance reuse and multi-API workflows" --description "Fix tests that create and use Hono apps, ensuring fresh instances per test.

Issues:
- Tests that reuse Hono app instances across test runs
- Multi-API endpoint tests with shared middleware/state
- Workflow tests combining multiple subsystems

Files to fix:
- packages/server/src/integration/workflows.test.ts (multi-API-endpoint workflows)
- packages/server/src/integration/user-workflows.test.ts (createApp, delay predictor, trip tracking)

Acceptance criteria:
- Each test creates a fresh Hono app instance
- No shared middleware or state between tests
- Multi-API workflow tests properly isolate each endpoint" --label split-child
args.description: Create third child bead for Hono app reuse

output: bf-3ldqg

args.command:
br create --title "Stabilize remaining cross-component workflow tests" --description "Stabilize the remaining integration tests that combine multiple subsystems.

Issues:
- Multi-step workflow tests with potential timing issues
- Transfer engine integration
- Error propagation and health monitoring tests
- Feed pipeline and delay prediction integration
- Observability and push subscription tests

Files to fix:
- packages/server/src/integration/commute-workflow.test.ts (multi-step commute analysis)
- packages/server/src/integration/commute-analysis.test.ts (transfer engine integration)
- packages/server/src/integration/error-propagation.test.ts
- packages/server/src/integration/health-monitoring.test.ts
- packages/server/src/integration/feed-pipeline.test.ts
- packages/server/src/integration/delay-prediction-integration.test.ts
- packages/server/src/integration/observability.test.ts
- packages/server/src/integration/push-subscriptions.test.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- All cross-component tests properly reset state between tests
- Multi-step workflows have proper async handling
- All remaining integration tests pass consistently on 3+ consecutive runs" --label split-child
args.description: Create fourth child bead for remaining workflow tests

output: bf-ihte5

content: Now let me wire up the dependencies:

Added dependency: bf-28zxk depends on bf-3had3 (blocks)

content:
SPLIT_COMPLETE: Created 4 children, parent converted to umbrella

Children: bf-3had3, bf-28zxk, bf-3ldqg, bf-ihte5

The bead has been successfully split into 4 sequential child beads:

1. **bf-3had3** - Fix test isolation and state reset (foundational)
2. **bf-28zxk** - Fix time-based assertions with deterministic time
3. **bf-3ldqg** - Fix Hono app instance reuse and multi-API workflows
4. **bf-ihte5** - Stabilize remaining cross-component workflow tests

The parent bead **bf-4fvp2** is now an umbrella that depends on the last child (bf-ihte5), and all children are chained sequentially. Each child can be completed independently, with clear acceptance criteria.
